item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Stop Wall Street Looting Act / Private Equity Transparency (S.2155)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Stop Wall Street Looting Act (S.2155)

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

S.2155 in the 119th Congress would impose transparency, accountability, and limit-of-extraction provisions on private equity (PE) firms, addressing the documented pattern of PE acquisitions leading to bankruptcies in healthcare, retail, housing, and journalism.

What does it actually do?

  • PE transparency: Requires disclosure of PE ownership stakes in operating companies.
  • Joint-and-several liability: PE firms bear liability for the debts of acquired companies (closes the bankruptcy-shield loophole).
  • Healthcare PE: Specific provisions for PE-owned hospitals, nursing homes, and physician practices.
  • Worker protections: Severance and pension protections in PE-driven bankruptcies.
  • Carried-interest taxation: Closes the carried-interest tax loophole.

Who votes when?

  • Senate Banking + HELP: Active consideration
  • House companion: Pending introduction
  • President: Signature dependent on chamber action

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes directly with the cooperative-architecture thesis. Liana Banyan exists structurally as the alternative to extractive financial architectures — including PE-driven rollups in healthcare (#6 Tatiana Schlossberg Health Accords + #7 MSA), retail (#3 Lets Go Shopping cooperative marketplace), and food (#1 Lets Make Dinner + #2 Lets Get Groceries cooperative grocery model).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Whistleblower Protection Expansion
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Whistleblower Protection Expansion

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Expansion of federal whistleblower protections in the 119th Congress, addressing gaps in current law for federal employees, contractors, and private-sector whistleblowers reporting fraud, waste, or abuse.

What does it actually do?

  • Federal employee protections — strengthens Merit Systems Protection Board enforcement
  • Contractor protections — covers federal contractor employees disclosing fraud
  • Anti-retaliation — increases damages and remedies for retaliation
  • Reporting channels — expands acceptable disclosure channels
  • Intelligence-community — limited expansion of IC whistleblower channels

Who votes when?

  • House Oversight + Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with the Federal-Congressional Body Cam transparency doctrine and the cooperative’s broader structural commitment to “accountability is dignity, not vulnerability.” Whistleblower protections are infrastructure for the transparency the cooperative argues for.
